pi_items.source: task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'Create a generator named "Nutrition ID Sequence"'); verified in setup_task.sh (line 48 deletes by this name), export_result.sh (line 29 WHERE clause matches this name), and verifier.py (line 66 checks generator existence with this name)

task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'for the Nutrition ID identifier type'); verified in setup_task.sh (lines 28-40 creates type with this exact name), export_result.sh (line 28 joins with patient_identifier_type), and verifier.py (line 85 checks target identifier type)

task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'Prefix: NUT'); verified in OpenMRS idgen SequentialIdentifierGenerator which supports 'prefix' property (confirmed via GitHub openmrs-module-idgen repository); verified in export_result.sh (line 20 selects g.prefix from idgen_seq_id_gen), and verifier.py (line 94 checks target prefix)

task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'Min Length: 6'); verified in OpenMRS idgen schema which includes min_length column in idgen_seq_id_gen table (confirmed via export_result.sh schema); verified in export_result.sh (line 21 selects g.min_length) and verifier.py (line 109 checks target min length)

task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'Max Length: 6'); verified in OpenMRS idgen schema which includes max_length column in idgen_seq_id_gen table (confirmed via export_result.sh schema); verified in export_result.sh (line 22 selects g.max_length) and verifier.py (line 110 checks target max length)

task.json metadata and description (line 5: 'Base Character Set: 0123456789'); verified in OpenMRS idgen SequentialIdentifierGenerator which supports 'baseCharacterSet' property (confirmed via GitHub openmrs-module-idgen repository); verified in export_result.sh (line 23 selects g.base_character_set from idgen_seq_id_gen), and verifier.py (line 120 checks target base set)

privileged_info_summary: The task configures an OpenMRS Sequential Identifier Generator with the following verified specifications: generator name 'Nutrition ID Sequence', linked to identifier type 'Nutrition ID', with a static 'NUT' prefix, base character set of digits 0-9 only, and fixed 6-digit minimum and maximum length constraints. All configuration parameters are consistent with OpenMRS idgen module properties as documented in the openmrs-module-idgen GitHub repository. The identifier type is created by setup_task.sh via the OpenMRS REST API if it does not already exist. The data is entirely synthetic (test fixture), created in a Docker-based Bahmni/OpenMRS environment. No real patient records or sensitive health information are involved.
